compiled_state.title: [worktree-integration]: fix-bug and debug-everything dispatch isolation:worktree without a harvest/integration path -> agent results orphaned and cleanup impossible
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context

When /dso:fix-bug or /dso:debug-everything dispatches a sub-agent with isolation: "worktree", the fix lands in an isolated git worktree at .claude/worktrees/agent-<id>, but neither skill has an integration step. The orchestrator returns to its own CWD and runs Step 7 (Verify), gates, and Step 8 (Commit) against the session tree — which never received the fix. Today the workflow only completes by accident (bug be6c-ed89's intermittent CWD-leak) or by developers not using isolation in practice. Without intervention, the recent fix to harvest-worktree.sh (shipped in afdb-8418) has no caller in these paths, so the cleanup-after-harvest guarantee is dead code for single-agent flows.

Sprint handles this cleanly via per-worktree-review-commit.md: orchestrator enters the agent worktree, runs review + test-status + commit inside it, then harvests. fix-bug and debug-everything need an analogous — but single-agent-shaped — protocol.

## Success Criteria

- SC1: /dso:fix-bug and /dso:debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode, run with worktree.isolation_enabled=true, produce a session-branch commit containing the fix and leave zero .claude/worktrees/agent-* entries and zero worktree-agent-* branches.
- SC2: fix-bug's post-fix gates (Step 7 Verify, Step 7.1 Scope-Drift, Gates 2a-2d, Step 7.5 Anti-Pattern Scan, Step 7.6 Test-Index) execute inside the agent worktree against the pre-commit working-tree diff — mirroring sprint's per-worktree-review-commit.md pattern. The diff the gates see is the diff that is committed.
- SC3: plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/single-agent-integrate.md exists, defines the enter-worktree → gates → record-test-status → commit → harvest sequence for single-agent flows, and is referenced by fix-bug/SKILL.md and debug-everything/SKILL.md at the post-fix-sub-agent-dispatch integration point.
- SC4: Dispatch templates (task-execution.md and the equivalent dispatch points in fix-bug/debug-everything) include a mandatory {orchestrator_root} placeholder. A pre-dispatch check fails closed when the placeholder is absent or unsubstituted.
- SC5: After every isolated dispatch in the affected skills, the orchestrator verifies the Agent result's worktreePath does NOT equal its own git root. Mismatch-failure (worktreePath == orchestrator_root) quarantines the sub-agent's outputs (does not harvest, surfaces to user or records INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED).
- SC6: Walking the be6c-ed89 incident with SC4+SC5 active would detect the observed leak before files landed on the session branch — confirmed via a deliberate test dispatch that reproduces the leak's signature (missing ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT substitution) and shows the pre-dispatch check blocks it.

## Dependencies

Hard prerequisite: afdb-8418 (shipped) — harvest-worktree.sh post-merge cleanup is load-bearing for SC1.
Related: be6c-ed89 (folded in; closable when SC5+SC6 land).

## Approach

Option A — extract plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/single-agent-integrate.md as a trimmed, single-agent variant of per-worktree-review-commit.md. fix-bug and debug-everything reference it at the post-fix-sub-agent-dispatch step. Defense-in-depth (template {orchestrator_root} enforcement + post-dispatch worktreePath verification) adds independently as small, testable increments.

## Investigation Evidence (empirical, this session)

Platform isolation works reliably. Side-by-side test: isolated dispatch produced sub-agent PWD and git root at .claude/worktrees/agent-<id>, canary file landed inside the isolated worktree and NOT in the orchestrator's session tree; control (no isolation) landed in orchestrator tree as expected. Agent result metadata includes worktreePath and worktreeBranch — the orchestrator has the information needed for SC5.

ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT is not auto-exported by the platform; it must be explicitly substituted into each dispatch prompt. Commit 65ffaa44 removed the only pre-dispatch enforcement layer (hook_worktree_isolation_guard); no current hook validates isolation mode at the dispatch boundary. SC4 + SC5 close this gap.

## Scenario Analysis (from pre-spec dialogue)

Three structural approaches were walked through inline before the spec was drafted:
- C1 (drop isolation from single-agent flows): rejected — loses blast-radius isolation benefit; weakens worktree.isolation_enabled semantics.
- C2 (new single-agent integrate protocol): selected — matches platform contract; mirrors sprint; leverages afdb-8418's harvest cleanup.
- C3 (fall-through to shared-directory when isolation_enabled=true): rejected — config-key meaning inconsistency.

Scenario A (compose fix-bug with per-worktree-review-commit.md as-is): rejected — sequence collision (per-worktree commits before gates; fix-bug gates expect working-tree diff).
Scenario B (cleanup-only shared reference): rejected — no integration call to clean up after.
Scenario C / Option A (single-agent-integrate shared prompt): accepted.

## Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered — no bright-line condition fired (no external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel pattern, performance, or migration concerns).
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): not triggered — graceful degradation; adversarial walkthrough of Scenarios A, B, C completed inline during pre-spec dialogue.
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none.
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered.
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered.
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): not triggered.

## Considerations (from /dso:preplanning --lightweight risk scan)

- [Testing] LLM-behavioral changes to fix-bug/debug-everything SKILL.md integration points — require eval-based verification or behavioral assertions per the llm-behavioral exemption. The SC4 validation hook and SC5 orchestrator verification logic are code (not LLM guidance) and follow standard TDD with unit tests.
- [Reliability] SC5 quarantine is a new failure mode — non-interactive mode must record INTERACTIVITY_DEFERRED instead of blocking. If the platform omits worktreePath in the Agent result (older harness versions), default to no-verification (graceful degradation) rather than fail-closed.
- [Maintainability] single-agent-integrate.md is a third per-worktree-style prompt alongside per-worktree-review-commit.md. Document the delta (single-agent vs. batch, conflict-queue presence/absence) so sprint's batch protocol and the single-agent protocol do not drift.

## Scope

**IN**:
- New shared prompt: plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/single-agent-integrate.md
- Integration-point edits in fix-bug/SKILL.md and debug-everything/SKILL.md referencing the shared prompt at post-fix-sub-agent-dispatch step
- Template enforcement: {orchestrator_root} placeholder mandatory in task-execution.md and equivalents; pre-dispatch validation fails closed when absent/unsubstituted
- Post-dispatch verification: orchestrator compares Agent result worktreePath against its own git root; mismatch-failure quarantines outputs
- Tests: unit tests for the pre-dispatch validation hook and the post-dispatch verification logic; behavioral assertion or eval for the skill-level integration references

**OUT**:
- Sprint's per-worktree-review-commit.md flow (already correct)
- harvest-worktree.sh post-merge cleanup (shipped in afdb-8418)
- worktree-cleanup.sh agent reclamation (shipped in afdb-8418)
- Platform-level changes to the Agent tool

comments.body: Brainstorm complete. Complexity: MODERATE (scope_certainty: High). Routing: /dso:preplanning --lightweight per brainstorm Phase 3 Step 4b. Session-signal override: not triggered (SC=6, scenarios=0).
